quotes.text: I had about two bites because it was straight up chocolate cake, and I can't do that for breakfast.
quotes.speaker: Katie Ledecky
quotes.context: Swimmer describing trying the 'infamous' chocolate muffins in the 2024 Paris Olympic Village, as quoted by AOL/People

source.title: Katie Ledecky Says She Tried the 'Infamous' Chocolate Muffins at 2024 Olympics: 'I Can't Do That for Breakfast'

quotes.text: I'm tired of hearing about money, money, money, money, money. I just want to play the game, drink Pepsi, wear Reebok.
quotes.speaker: Shaquille O'Neal
quotes.context: NBA center, in July 1996 after leaving the Orlando Magic for the Lakers, when asked about contract money

source.title: 'I Just Want To Play The Game, Drink Pepsi, Wear Reebok': Remembering The Time Shaquille O'Neal Dropped Random Knowledge

quotes.text: I've never actually owned a telescope. I just describe space in a way that makes people assume I have one nearby.
quotes.speaker: Neil deGrasse Tyson
quotes.context: Supposedly at a Q&A after a public lecture, 2023
quotes.fake_note: Fits his self-aware, slightly theatrical persona perfectly. The tell is that Tyson famously does own and use telescopes and would never frame his expertise as a performance.

quotes.text: Well, it pissed a few people off and I figured it was worth doing.
quotes.speaker: Willie Nelson
quotes.context: Explaining why he stuck with his iconic long braids, in an interview with Larry King (as reported by Whiskey Riff)

source.title: “It Pissed A Few People Off, And I Figured It Was Worth Doing” — Willie Nelson On Why He’s Stuck With His Iconic Braids | Whiskey Riff

quotes.text: People ask how I stay motivated. Honestly? I just really, deeply hate losing to myself. Past-me set a record and present-me finds that offensive.
quotes.speaker: Serena Williams
quotes.context: Supposedly in a 2019 Nike promotional podcast
quotes.fake_note: The competitive self-rivalry narrative matches her documented mindset. The tell is the oddly comedic framing — she's spoken intensely about competition but not quite this self-deprecatingly absurd.

quotes.text: The secret to a perfect risotto is exactly 22 minutes of stirring. Not 20. Not 25. Twenty-two. I set a timer and I leave the room so I'm not tempted to rush.
quotes.speaker: Gordon Ramsay
quotes.context: Supposedly in a 2022 MasterChef behind-the-scenes interview
quotes.fake_note: The obsessive precision is totally on-brand for Ramsay. The tell is that professional risotto technique deliberately varies timing based on heat and rice variety — no real chef would give a single fixed number.
